messages.content: setup the infrastructure for this project. if possible run as parallel agents.
Let me check the current state before spinning up parallel agents.
Docker isn't installed. I need to install it first, then set up the infrastructure. Let me also check for API keys in the environment.
No API keys found in the environment, and Docker isn't installed. Let me install Docker first, then run the rest in parallel. This requires sudo.

I can't run `sudo` directly. You'll need to install Docker yourself. Run this in your terminal:

```bash
! sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl gnupg && \
  sudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings && \
  curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg && \
  sudo chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg && \
  echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null && \
  sudo apt-get update -qq && \
  sudo apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin && \
  sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
```

While you do that, let me prepare the `.env` file and review the `docker-compose.yml` so we're ready to go immediately after.
Now I have everything I need. Let me create the `.env` file and get the LAN IP while you install Docker.

`.env` is created. Here's the full setup plan:

**Step 1 — Install Docker** (you need to run this yourself):
```bash
! sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl gnupg && \
  sudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings && \
  curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg && \
  sudo chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg && \
  echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu noble stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null && \
  sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin && \
  sudo usermod -aG docker $USER && newgrp docker
```

**Step 2 — Fill in API keys** in `.env`:
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — for Mem0 and Cognee LLM
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY` — for Graphiti (via Google AI Studio OpenAI-compatible endpoint) and Cognee embeddings
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` — for Mem0 embeddings (or set `EMBEDDER_PROVIDER=ollama` to avoid needing this)

**Step 3 — Start services** (once Docker is installed and keys are set):
```bash
! cd /home/tomas/agentic-memory-benchmarking && docker compose pull && docker compose up -d
```

**Step 4 — Run preflight check**:
```bash
! cd /home/tomas/agentic-memory-benchmarking && bash preflight.sh
```

**Step 5 — Configure MCP on participant machines** (using host IP `192.168.0.44`):
```bash
bash setup-mcp.sh 192.168.0.44
```

---

**Services that will be running:**

| Service | Port | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| PostgreSQL (pgvector) | 5432 | Shared DB for Cognee + eval results |
| Qdrant | 6333, 6334 | Vector store for Mem0 |
| Neo4j | 7474 (UI), 7687 | Graph DB for Graphiti |
| Mem0 MCP | 8181 | `/sse` endpoint |
| Graphiti MCP | 8050 | `/sse` endpoint |
| Cognee MCP | 8000 | `/mcp/sse` endpoint |
| File server | 9000 | Serves `shared-data/` |

Let me know once Docker is installed and the keys are ready — I'll launch parallel agents to pull images and bring everything up simultaneously.
